commands.review_summary:
The PR changes browser attachment completion detection to allow a stable ready native file-input basename match when ChatGPT exposes no chip or count evidence.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main rejects the linked ready/inputNames/no-chip/no-count state because the existing fallback requires separate attachment/upload/count evidence. I did not run a live signed-in browser smoke during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real browser proof and a small predicate fix; automation should not take over because it cannot provide the contributor's live setup proof.

target.title: [codex] Fix browser attachment input readiness
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the browser attachment readiness fallback for the #275 state where ChatGPT exposes expected file names through the native file input, the composer is ready, but no chip/count attachment evidence is visible.
- Adds a narrow input-only completion path guarded by exact expected basenames, ready send state, no active upload signal, no chip/count evidence, and stable input names.
- Keeps disabled/missing/uploading states from completing through the new fallback and removes uploading as standalone evidence in the older input fallback.

Fixes #275.

## Validation

- Oracle patch-plan review: no blocker; recommended exact input-name + ready/non-uploading input-only fallback.
- Oracle final diff review: `APPROVE`, no concrete correctness or test blocker.
- `pnpm vitest run tests/browser/attachmentsCompletion.test.ts tests/browser/pageActions.test.ts`
- `pnpm run check`
target.author: wangwllu
